a b : ℝ
⊢ ∫ (x : ℝ) in a..b, 2 * x * exp (x ^ 2) = exp (b ^ 2) - exp (a ^ 2)

proof:
have h₁ : ∀ (x : ℝ), HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (2 * x * Real.exp (x ^ 2)) x := by

      simpa using (hasDerivAt_pow 2 x)
    have h₃ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (Real.exp (x ^ 2) * (2 * x)) x := by
      have h₄ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => x ^ 2) (2 * x) x := h₂
      have h₅ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (Real.exp (x ^ 2) * (2 * x)) x := by
        apply HasDerivAt.exp h₄
      exact h₅
    convert h₃ using 1 <;> ring_nf <;>
      simp [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
    <;>
      linarith
  have h₂ : (∫ x in a..b, 2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = exp (b ^ 2) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
    have h₃ : ∫ x in a..b, 2 * x * exp (x ^ 2) = exp (b ^ 2) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
      -- Use the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus to evaluate the integral
      have h₄ : ∫ x in a..b, 2 * x * exp (x ^ 2) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
        -- Use the fact that the derivative of exp(x^2) is 2x exp(x^2)
        have h₅ : ∫ x in a..b, 2 * x * exp (x ^ 2) = ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) := rfl
        rw [h₅]
        -- Apply the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
        have h₆ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = exp (b ^ 2) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
          -- Use the integral version of the FTC
          have h₇ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
            -- The function exp(x^2) is differentiable everywhere
            have h₈ : ∀ x : ℝ, HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (2 * x * Real.exp (x ^ 2)) x := h₁
            -- Use the FTC to evaluate the integral
            have h₉ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
              -- Apply the integral version of the FTC
              have h₁₀ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                -- Use the fact that the derivative of exp(x^2) is 2x exp(x^2)
                have h₁₁ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                  -- Use the interval integral version of the FTC
                  have h₁₂ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                    -- Use the fact that the derivative of exp(x^2) is 2x exp(x^2)
                    have h₁₃ : ∀ x ∈ Set.Icc a b, HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (2 * x * Real.exp (x ^ 2)) x := by
                      intro x hx
                      exact h₁ x
                    -- Use the interval integral version of the FTC
                    have h₁₄ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                      -- Use the interval integral version of the FTC
                      have h₁₅ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                        -- Apply the FTC
                        have h₁₆ : ∫ x in a..b, (2 * x * exp (x ^ 2)) = (exp (b ^ 2)) - exp (a ^ 2) := by
                          -- Use the interval integral version of the FTC
                          apply intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt
                          · intro x hx
                            have h₁₇ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ => Real.exp (x ^ 2)) (2 * x * Real.exp (x ^ 2)) x := h₁ x
                            convert h₁₇ using 1 <;>
                              simp [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
                          · -- Continuity of the integrand
                            apply Continuous.intervalIntegrable
                            continuity
                        exact h₁₆
                      exact h₁₅
                    exact h₁₄
                  exact h₁₂
                exact h₁₁
              exact h₁₀
            exact h₉
          exact h₇
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    exact h₃
  exact h₂
